relationshipMesh.relationship: Plant intelligence includes chemical signaling, root communication, electrical activity, defense response, sensory biology, cooperation, memory, and adaptation.
Plant communication integrates with electrical signaling through membrane voltage, ion channels, action potentials, variation potentials, calcium waves, and wound responses.
Plant electrophysiology organizes electrical messages through voltage change, ion movement, calcium signaling, tissue conduction, and environmental response.
Action potentials are rapid electrical pulses supporting fast plant responses, movement, defense signaling, and signal transmission.
Variation potentials are slower systemic electrical responses associated with wounds, stress, hydraulic change, and whole-plant coordination.
Membrane voltage depends upon ion gradients while ion channels regulate the movement of ions responsible for electrical signaling.
Ion channels enable electrical signals to propagate throughout roots, stems, leaves, and vascular tissues.
Electrical signals coordinate wound response, volatile release, defense chemistry, immune activation, and protection from herbivory.
Plant electrical activity integrates with underground fungal-root communication systems responsible for nutrient movement, signaling, and ecosystem connectivity.
Mycorrhizal networks extend plant signaling into ecosystem-scale electrical interactions involving soils, microbes, water, biodiversity, and carbon.

Electrical Ecology connects Plant Electrophysiologyâ„˘, Mycorrhizal Networksâ„˘, Soil Systemsâ„˘, Water Systemsâ„˘, Bioelectric Systemsâ„˘, Carbon & Microbial Lifeâ„˘, and Information Systems in Nature into a unified semantic retrieval network.
